heroCompanyDescription: WIA Gold Limited (ASX: WIA) is an ASX-listed junior gold exploration company operating in the Basic Materials / Gold sector. The company is at an early stage of its corporate lifecycle, having recently completed a capital raise as evidenced by a Cleansing Notice and multiple unquoted securities notifications filed on the ASX in early March 2026. No revenue, EBITDA, resource estimates, or operational project disclosures are publicly available, confirming WIA is a pre-revenue exploration entity with an information-constrained public profile.

skew.rationale: The probability skew is modestly neutral-to-cautious at initiation. While the gold price environment is exceptionally supportive at US$5,027, the near-total absence of operational disclosures, broker coverage, and financial data prevents a confident bullish skew. The base case (N2) carries the highest weight at 45%, reflecting the most likely scenario of an early-stage explorer consolidating after a capital raise, with the bear case (N3) carrying meaningful weight at 28% given the information vacuum.

verdict.text: At A$0.52, WIA Gold is priced as a speculative gold exploration option in one of the most supportive gold price environments in history, with gold at US$5,018/oz. The market is paying a substantial premium over any identifiable asset value, betting that the recently completed capital raise will be deployed into a project capable of generating market-moving results. However, the complete absence of operational disclosures — no drill results, no resource estimates, no project descriptions — means the entire re-rating from A$0.14 is attributable to sector sentiment rather than company-specific fundamentals. The April 2026 Quarterly Activities Report is now the critical near-term test: if it discloses active exploration with technical data, the current price becomes more defensible; if it contains only administrative content, the speculative premium is at significant risk of unwinding toward the A$0.25–0.35 range.

featuredRationale: The four hypotheses are weighted with N2 (Base Case, 45%) as the dominant scenario, reflecting the most probable outcome of a consolidating junior explorer. N3 (Bear Case, 28%) carries the second-highest weight given the significant information gaps and absence of operational news. N1 (Bull Case, 20%) reflects the genuine optionality created by record gold prices and recent capital raising. N4 (Tail Catalyst, 7%) captures the low-probability but high-impact scenario of a transformational corporate event.

identity.overview: WIA Gold Limited (ASX: WIA) — auto-added to coverage. Full company overview pending analyst research.

hypotheses.title: N1: Growth/Recovery
hypotheses.description: With gold trading at US$5,018/oz, WIA's exploration assets could attract significant re-rating interest if the company announces a material resource update or drill result. Recent capital raising activity and director interest notices suggest the company is actively deploying funds toward exploration work, and a positive discovery at current gold prices could drive a substantial re-rating. This scenario remains plausible but unconfirmed.
supporting: Gold price at US$5,027 (+3.0% today) represents a historically strong macro backdrop for junior gold explorers
Stock has already re-rated sharply from a 52-week low of A$0.14 to A$0.52, suggesting the market is beginning to price in exploration optionality
Recent capital raising (Cleansing Notice, unquoted securities issuances) indicates the company has secured fresh funding to advance its project pipeline
Director interest notices suggest board members are actively engaged with the company at current price levels
contradicting: No market-sensitive operational announcements have been made, meaning there is no confirmed exploration progress to underpin a re-rating
Absence of broker coverage limits institutional awareness and potential for a sustained re-rating
No resource estimate, production data, or project economics are publicly available to anchor a bull case valuation

hypotheses.title: N2: Base Case
hypotheses.description: WIA Gold is most likely an early-stage gold explorer that has recently completed a capital raise and is in the process of deploying funds toward exploration activities. The stock trades near its 52-week high of A$0.58, reflecting gold price enthusiasm at US$5,018/oz rather than company-specific news, and is likely to consolidate until a substantive operational update is released. This scenario implies fair value in the A$0.35–0.45 range absent new catalysts.
supporting: Recent administrative filings (unquoted securities, Cleansing Notice) are consistent with a post-capital-raise consolidation phase typical of junior explorers
Stock price at A$0.52 is near the 52-week high of A$0.58, suggesting the market has already partially priced in the gold price tailwind
No earnings, revenue, or EBITDA data available, consistent with a pre-revenue exploration company
Director interest notices indicate governance activity consistent with a company preparing for the next phase of operations
contradicting: The absence of any operational update or project announcement makes it difficult to confirm the company is actively progressing exploration
Gold at US$5,027 creates pressure for management to communicate project status to capitalise on investor interest in the sector

hypotheses.title: N3: Risk/Downside
hypotheses.description: The complete absence of market-sensitive operational announcements, broker coverage, media attention, and financial disclosures continues to raise the risk that WIA is a shell or dormant explorer with limited near-term catalysts. If the recent capital raise was small and the company lacks a credible project pipeline, the current price of A$0.52 represents significant downside risk to the A$0.10–0.15 range. Continued administrative-only filings beyond mid-2026 would materially increase this scenario's probability.
supporting: Zero market-sensitive announcements in the observed period despite gold trading at record levels -- a credible explorer would typically be communicating project progress
No media or social coverage, no broker research, and no financial statements available, consistent with a company with minimal operational activity
Market cap listed as 'None' in available data, suggesting limited institutional tracking or data provider coverage
The 52-week low of A$0.14 demonstrates the stock is capable of severe drawdowns, and the current price may reflect speculative gold sentiment rather than fundamental value
contradicting: Recent capital raising activity suggests the company is not entirely dormant and has access to capital markets
Gold at US$5,027 provides a strong sector tailwind that may sustain speculative interest even without company-specific news
Director interest changes suggest board engagement, which is inconsistent with a fully dormant entity

hypotheses.title: N4: Disruption/Catalyst
hypotheses.description: Given the recent capital raising, director changes, and the absence of operational updates, there remains a tail probability that WIA is negotiating a transformational asset acquisition or reverse merger. At current gold prices of US$5,018/oz, a credible acquisition of a development-stage or producing asset could trigger a dramatic re-rating well above A$1.00. This scenario is assigned low probability given the lack of any confirming disclosure, but the corporate activity pattern keeps it alive.
supporting: The combination of a Cleansing Notice, unquoted securities issuances, and a new director appointment is a pattern sometimes associated with pre-acquisition capital structuring
Gold at US$5,027 makes acquisition of gold assets highly strategic and value-accretive at current prices
The stock's sharp re-rating from A$0.14 to A$0.52 could reflect informed buying ahead of a material announcement
contradicting: No market-sensitive announcement has been made, and ASX continuous disclosure obligations would require prompt disclosure of any material transaction
The absence of any media or broker commentary makes a near-term transformational deal less likely
This scenario is highly speculative given the complete lack of supporting evidence in available disclosures

narrative.theNarrative: [Updated 17-Mar-26 20:55 AEST] WIA Gold Limited continues to trade as a pure speculative gold sector option at A$0.52, with the investment case driven almost entirely by the extraordinary macro backdrop rather than company-specific fundamentals. Gold is trading at US$5,018 per ounce, up 2.8% on the day, maintaining one of the most supportive commodity price environments in history for ASX-listed gold explorers. The company's recent ASX filings — including unquoted securities notifications dated 17 March and 9 March 2026, a Change of Director's Interest Notice on 12 March 2026, an Initial Director's Interest Notice, and a Cleansing Notice on 9 March 2026 — confirm that a capital raise has been completed, but provide zero operational content about exploration activities, project locations, or use of funds. The stock has re-rated approximately 271% from its 52-week low of A$0.14 and is trading near its 52-week high of A$0.58, a move that significantly outpaces even the strong gold price appreciation over the same period. No drill results, resource estimates, financial statements, or project disclosures have been released, meaning the entire re-rating is attributable to sector sentiment and speculative positioning. The next material catalyst remains the April 2026 Quarterly Activities Report, which will be the first opportunity to assess whether the recently raised capital is being deployed into a credible exploration program.

priceImplication.label: Embedded Assumptions at A$0.52
priceImplication.content:
At A$0.52, the current price appears to embed:

• A meaningful probability that WIA holds a credible gold exploration asset capable of generating a resource estimate at current gold prices of US$5,018/oz

• An assumption that the recently completed capital raise is sufficient to fund a near-term exploration program with market-moving results

• A gold price of approximately US$4,500–5,000/oz remaining supportive throughout the exploration cycle

• A low probability that WIA is a dormant shell with no active exploration program, despite the complete absence of operational disclosures

• An implicit expectation of a market-sensitive announcement — drill results, resource estimate, or asset acquisition — within the next 3–6 months

• At A$0.52, the stock is trading at a significant premium to any identifiable net asset value, implying the market is paying almost entirely for optionality and sector sentiment

narrative.evidenceCheck: The evidence base remains extremely thin and represents one of the most information-constrained coverage situations possible for an ASX-listed company. The only verified data points are administrative ASX filings — unquoted securities notifications, a Cleansing Notice, and director interest notices — and the gold spot price at US$5,018/oz. No financial statements, resource estimates, drill results, project descriptions, or operational updates have been released. The evidence strongly supports the N3 (Bear/Risk) scenario in terms of information quality, but the gold macro backdrop and the pattern of corporate activity (capital raise completion, director changes) provide circumstantial support for N2 (Base Case) and a small probability weight for N1 (Bull) and N4 (Disruption). The evidence does not support the current price on fundamental grounds — the re-rating from A$0.14 to A$0.52 is entirely sentiment-driven and unsupported by any company-specific operational disclosure.
narrative.narrativeStability: The current narrative — speculative gold sector optionality on an undisclosed exploration asset — remains inherently unstable because it is built almost entirely on macro sentiment rather than company-specific fundamentals. The narrative has not been tested by any operational disclosure to date. It could be positively destabilised by a single market-sensitive announcement: a drill result, resource estimate, or asset acquisition that confirms WIA holds a credible project. Conversely, it could be negatively destabilised by continued silence beyond the April 2026 Quarterly Activities Report, which would increase the probability that WIA is a dormant explorer and push the N3 bear case weight above 40%. The narrative now rests critically on the April 2026 Quarterly Activities Report as the next scheduled opportunity for operational disclosure — if that report contains only administrative content with no exploration activity, the speculative premium embedded in the current price becomes increasingly difficult to justify.

evidence.intro: Evidence is assessed across ten epistemic domains, ranked by reliability. Each domain carries a distinct epistemic quality — from verified regulatory filings to unverified media commentary — ensuring the analyst understands what the evidence is before assessing what it means.

cards.title: 1. Corporate Communications
cards.finding: WIA Gold Limited has not issued any market-sensitive operational or strategic announcements in the provided period (March 2026). Recent communications are limited to notifications regarding unquoted securities and director interest changes, indicating a focus on capital structure and governance rather than project updates.
cards.tension: The absence of strategic updates or project-specific news makes it difficult to assess the company's current operational focus or future growth plans, potentially indicating a lack of significant recent developments or a deliberate strategy to not disclose early-stage information.

cards.title: 2. Regulatory Filings & Financial Statements
cards.finding: WIA Gold Limited has recently filed multiple notifications regarding unquoted securities (2026-03-17, 2026-03-09) and a Cleansing Notice (2026-03-09), indicating recent capital raising or issuance activities. Additionally, Director's Interest Notices were filed on 2026-03-12 and 2026-03-09.
cards.tension: While capital raising can fund growth, the specific terms, use of proceeds, and potential dilutive impact on existing shareholders are not detailed in the provided snippets. The absence of full financial statements limits understanding of the company's financial health and operational performance.

cards.title: 3. Broker Research
cards.finding: No broker research or consensus estimates for WIA Gold Limited are available in the provided data.
cards.tension: The absence of broker coverage suggests the company may be under-researched, potentially due to its size, early stage, or lack of significant market-moving news, making independent valuation and understanding of market expectations more challenging.

cards.title: 4. Competitor Disclosures
cards.finding: No specific competitor disclosures or comparative market positioning data for WIA Gold Limited are provided. The company operates in the Basic Materials / Gold sector, implying a competitive landscape with other gold explorers and producers.
cards.tension: Without peer comparison, it is difficult to assess WIA's relative operational efficiency, project pipeline, or valuation against other gold explorers or producers in the market, hindering a comprehensive competitive analysis.

cards.title: 5. Economic Data
cards.finding: The gold price (GC=F) is currently US$5018.0, showing a significant increase of +2.8% today. This represents a strong and supportive macro environment for gold mining companies, potentially boosting revenue and project economics.
cards.tension: While the high gold price is a positive tailwind, WIA's specific project economics, production costs, and hedging strategies (if any) are unknown, meaning the direct benefit to the company's profitability cannot be fully quantified without further operational details.

cards.title: 6. Alternative Data
cards.finding: No alternative data, such as operational metrics (e.g., production volumes, exploration results not in ASX announcements), ESG performance, or channel checks, is available for WIA Gold Limited.
cards.tension: The lack of such data limits insights into the company's on-the-ground operational progress, environmental and social impact, or community relations, which are increasingly important for mining projects and investor sentiment.

cards.title: 7. Academic Research
cards.finding: No specific academic research or industry studies pertaining directly to WIA Gold Limited, its projects, or its operating region are provided.
cards.tension: Academic insights into geological potential, mining techniques, or regional resource trends could offer valuable context for WIA's exploration and development prospects, which is currently missing and could inform long-term strategic assessments.

cards.title: 8. Media & Social
cards.finding: There are no recent news headlines or social media commentary provided for WIA Gold Limited.
cards.tension: The absence of media coverage might indicate a low level of public interest or significant market-moving events, potentially limiting investor awareness, liquidity, and the company's ability to communicate its story to a broader audience.

cards.title: 9. Leadership & Governance
cards.finding: Recent ASX filings include a 'Change of Director's Interest Notice' (2026-03-12) and an 'Initial Director's Interest Notice' (2026-03-09), indicating recent changes in director shareholdings or new director appointments, suggesting active board involvement.
cards.tension: While these notices suggest active board involvement, the specific details of the changes (e.g., director buying/selling, new director's background) and their implications for company strategy or governance are not provided, making it difficult to assess their impact on future performance.

cards.title: 10. Ownership & Capital Flows
cards.finding: WIA Gold Limited has recently issued unquoted securities (2026-03-17, 2026-03-09) and filed a Cleansing Notice (2026-03-09), suggesting recent capital raising activities. The stock has seen significant price appreciation, trading at A$0.52, near its 52-week high of A$0.58, up from a low of A$0.14.
cards.tension: The market cap is stated as 'None', which is a significant data gap, making it impossible to assess the company's overall valuation or scale. While the price appreciation is positive, the dilutive impact of recent capital raises and the identity of new investors are not detailed, creating uncertainty about the capital structure.

evidence.alignmentSummary: Evidence presents a dichotomy: strong macro tailwinds from the gold price are offset by a complete absence of company-specific operational news, leading to significant information gaps regarding WIA's project activity and financial health.

discriminators.intro: The following data points carry high diagnosticity — they can meaningfully shift probability between competing hypotheses.

rows.evidence: First market-sensitive operational announcement (drill result, resource estimate, or asset acquisition): This is the single most diagnostic data point available. Its presence or absence, and its content, will discriminate sharply between N1 (bull), N2 (base), and N3 (bear) scenarios.
rows.currentReading: No market-sensitive operational announcement has been made as of 2026-03-17. Recent ASX filings are administrative (capital structure, director interests) and provide no operational content. This is a negative signal for N1 and a supporting signal for N3.

rows.evidence: March Quarter Activities Report cash expenditure and operational activity: The quantum and nature of cash spent on exploration in Q1 2026 will reveal whether the company is genuinely active or dormant. Exploration expenditure above A$500,000 in the quarter would be a meaningful positive signal.
rows.currentReading: Not yet available -- due April 2026. Current evidence is ambiguous; capital raise confirms cash availability but operational deployment is unconfirmed.

rows.evidence: Gold price trajectory (US$/oz): At US$5,027, gold is providing maximum sector tailwind. A sustained move above US$5,200 would amplify N1 probability; a correction below US$4,500 would reduce the speculative premium across all junior gold explorers and increase N3 probability for WIA specifically.
rows.currentReading: US$5,027 (+3.0% today) -- strongly supportive of N1 and N2 scenarios. This is the single most positive data point in the current evidence set.

discriminators.nonDiscriminating:
• ASX listing status: Confirms the company exists and is subject to continuous disclosure obligations, but does not discriminate between any of the four scenarios.

• Director interest notices: Indicate board engagement but are filed routinely and do not discriminate between an active explorer and a dormant shell.

• 52-week price range (A$0.14 - A$0.58): Demonstrates high volatility consistent with a junior explorer, but the range itself does not discriminate between the bull and bear cases going forward.

• Cleansing Notice filing: Confirms a capital raise occurred but does not discriminate between a company raising capital for genuine exploration versus one raising capital to fund corporate overhead.

• Sector classification (Basic Materials / Gold): Confirms gold price relevance but does not discriminate between a high-quality and low-quality exploration asset within the sector.

tripwires.intro: The following catalysts could force a material revision to hypothesis weightings. Each carries conditional outcomes that map to specific narrative shifts.

cards.name: Quarterly Activities Report (March Quarter)

conditions.if: If the March quarter activities report discloses active drilling, exploration expenditure, or a defined project area with technical data
conditions.then: Then N1 probability increases materially; stock likely re-rates toward A$0.65-0.80 as the market gains confidence in the exploration pipeline

conditions.if: If the March quarter report contains no operational activity, minimal cash expenditure, or confirms the company is still in a pre-exploration phase
conditions.then: Then N3 probability increases to 35-40%; stock likely retraces toward A$0.30-0.40 as the speculative premium erodes

cards.source: ASX Listing Rules -- quarterly reporting obligation for exploration entities

cards.name: Market-Sensitive Drill or Exploration Result

conditions.if: If WIA releases a market-sensitive announcement with positive drill intersections (e.g., >1g/t Au over meaningful widths) or a maiden resource estimate
conditions.then: Then N1 becomes the dominant scenario; at US$5,000+ gold, a credible resource could support a stock price of A$0.80-1.50 depending on scale

conditions.if: If no market-sensitive operational announcement is made by end of May 2026 despite having raised capital in March 2026
conditions.then: Then N3 bear case probability rises significantly; management credibility is questioned and the stock risks retracing toward A$0.20-0.30

cards.source: ASX continuous disclosure obligations; company exploration timeline

cards.name: Capital Structure Update / Further Raising

conditions.if: If WIA announces a further capital raise at or above A$0.44 with a credible use-of-funds statement tied to a specific exploration program
conditions.then: Then the base case (N2) is reinforced and the bull case (N1) probability increases; institutional participation would be a particularly positive signal

conditions.if: If WIA announces a capital raise at a significant discount to market (below A$0.40) or issues a large number of options/warrants suggesting financial stress
conditions.then: Then N3 bear case is confirmed; dilution and financial pressure would likely push the stock toward A$0.20-0.30 and erode the speculative premium

cards.source: ASX announcements; company capital management activity

cards.name: March Quarter Activities Report — Operational Content Test

conditions.if: If the March quarter report discloses active drilling, exploration expenditure above A$500k, or a defined project area with assay or geophysical data
conditions.then: Then N2 base case is confirmed and N1 probability increases to 30%+; current price of A$0.52 becomes more defensible and the stock could re-test the A$0.58 52-week high

conditions.if: If the March quarter report contains only administrative content, cash balance disclosure with minimal exploration spend, or no project-specific technical data
conditions.then: Then N3 bear case probability increases to 40%+, the speculative premium in the current price becomes unjustifiable, and a retracement toward A$0.25–0.35 becomes the base case

cards.source: ASX Listing Rules — Appendix 5B quarterly cash flow and activities report due within one month of quarter end (31 March 2026)

gaps.analyticalLimitations: This initiation is subject to severe analytical limitations arising from the near-complete absence of company-specific fundamental data. The analysis is based on fewer than five substantive data points, of which only the gold spot price carries high confidence. All scenario probabilities should be treated as highly provisional first estimates subject to material revision upon receipt of any operational disclosure. The absence of a market capitalisation figure prevents any form of enterprise value or per-ounce valuation analysis. The lack of broker coverage means there is no independent validation of management's strategy or project quality. Investors should treat this analysis as a framework for monitoring future developments rather than a basis for investment decisions, and should conduct their own due diligence including a review of the company's full ASX filing history, project technical reports, and management background before forming a view.

trend.structure: Price in uptrend relative to 50-day and 200-day moving averages.

footer.disclaimer: This report does not constitute personal financial advice. Continuum Intelligence synthesises cross-domain evidence using the Analysis of Competing Hypotheses (ACH) methodology. All factual claims are sourced from ASX filings, company disclosures, broker consensus data, and publicly available information.

hero.embedded_thesis: At A$0.52, the current price embeds a combination of gold sector enthusiasm and speculative optionality on an undisclosed exploration project, rather than any company-specific fundamental value. The stock's re-rating from a 52-week low of A$0.14 implies the market is pricing in a meaningful probability that WIA will announce a credible exploration result or asset acquisition in the near term. With gold at US$5,018/oz — near all-time highs — the macro backdrop provides a powerful justification for holding speculative junior gold positions, but the complete absence of operational disclosures means the current price is almost entirely sentiment-driven. At A$0.52, the market is effectively paying a significant premium over any identifiable asset value, betting that the recently completed capital raise will be deployed into a project capable of generating market-moving results.
hero.skew_description: The four hypotheses are weighted with N2 (Base Case, 45%) as the dominant scenario, reflecting the most probable outcome of a consolidating junior explorer awaiting its first operational catalyst. N3 (Bear Case, 28%) carries the second-highest weight given the significant information gaps and continued absence of operational news. N1 (Bull Case, 20%) and N4 (Disruption, 7%) together represent a 27% probability of a materially positive outcome, keeping the risk/reward skew modestly positive given the gold macro tailwind but firmly anchored by the information vacuum.

next_decision_point.metric: Disclosure of active drilling, exploration expenditure, defined project area with technical data, or any market-sensitive operational announcement
next_decision_point.thresholds: A quarterly report disclosing active drilling or a defined exploration program with technical data would support N1/N2 and maintain the current price level. A report containing only administrative content with no exploration activity would increase N3 probability to 40%+ and suggest a retracement toward A$0.25–0.35. A market-sensitive drill result or resource estimate released ahead of the quarterly would trigger N1 and could push the stock toward A$0.80–0.90. A transformational acquisition announcement would trigger N4 and could push the stock above A$1.00.

priceDrivers.primary_driver: WIA's 10-day decline from its 52-week high is driven by a combination of placement overhang following a confirmed capital raise (Cleansing Notice, 9 March 2026) and a broad gold equity sector correction from elevated levels, with the post-placement price path providing the more precise timing fit for the stock-specific portion of the move.

primary: Placement overhang from confirmed capital raise (Cleansing Notice 9 March 2026) — precise timing alignment with post-bounce decline and multi-day floor behaviour near A$0.44
Broad gold equity sector correction from 4 March 2026 peak — confirmed across NST, EVN and ASB, explains the initial A$0.58 to A$0.53 decline before the placement
secondary: Technical reversal from 52-week high of A$0.58 on approximately 4 March 2026, following an 11-month, +314% rally from A$0.14
ASX200 broad market decline of approximately 4–5% over the 10-day window contributing a portion of the absolute move
Gold spot price recovery (+2.77% on 17 March) providing support for the modest 2-day bounce of +2.0%
amplifiers: Technical resistance at 52-week high coinciding with the sector peak, accelerating initial selling
Absence of any positive company catalyst to arrest the decline during the correction window
background: WIA had rallied +314% from A$0.14 (April 2025) to A$0.58 (March 2026), creating an extended position vulnerable to sector or company-specific shocks
Gold spot price at US$5,018 — extraordinarily elevated, meaning gold equities broadly were priced for perfection
ASX200 in a declining trend throughout March 2026, down approximately 5% over the window
rejected: NST-specific event on 12 March 2026 as a sector contagion driver — directly contradicted by EVN declining only ~0.35% and ASB holding flat on that date; NST's -18.7% single-session collapse was company-specific and did not propagate as a broad gold sector event
Reported +202.94% single-day change — confirmed data artefact; price history shows A$0.44 on both 16 and 17 March 2026 with no discontinuity
Director interest notices as an independent price catalyst — administrative in nature, consistent with placement participation rather than a standalone signal; price declined after filing
Unquoted securities notifications as market-moving events — assessed as administrative, most likely relating to options or performance rights

report.title: WIA Gold: Placement Overhang and Sector Correction Drive -10.5% Pullback From 52-Week High
report.executive_summary: WIA Gold has declined 10.53% over the past 10 trading days, underperforming the ASX200 by 5.4 percentage points, following a peak at its 52-week high of A$0.58 on approximately 4 March 2026. The move is best explained by two near-simultaneous forces: a broad gold equity sector correction visible across peers NST, EVN and ASB, and placement overhang from a capital raise confirmed by a Cleansing Notice lodged on 9 March 2026. The placement hypothesis carries the more precise timing fit for WIA's stock-specific price path post-9 March — the stock bounced to A$0.56 on the cleansing notice date, then sold off 10.7% over three sessions to A$0.44, where it held as a floor for four consecutive days, consistent with placement participant selling and likely placement price support. The sector correction explains the earlier leg from A$0.58 to A$0.53. Confidence is moderate pending confirmation of placement price and size.
report.full_note:
WIA Gold has retraced 10.53% from its 52-week high over the past 10 trading days, closing at A$0.44 on 17 March 2026 after peaking at A$0.58 on approximately 4 March. This pullback follows an extraordinary 11-month rally of +314% from A$0.14, and occurred against a backdrop of both sector-wide gold equity selling and a company-specific capital raise event. The most likely primary driver of the post-9 March leg of the decline is placement overhang, with the broader sector correction driving the earlier portion.

The strongest supporting evidence for the placement overhang thesis is the precise timing alignment between the Cleansing Notice filed on 9 March 2026 and the subsequent price path. Under ASX Listing Rule 3.10.3, a Cleansing Notice is required after a placement to non-disclosure investors, confirming a capital raise was completed around that date. On the cleansing notice date itself, WIA bounced to A$0.56 — consistent with the placement price being set at or near that level. Over the following three sessions (10–12 March), the stock declined 10.7% to A$0.44, a move that is precisely consistent with placement participants receiving their securities and selling into available liquidity. Critically, A$0.44 then acted as a floor across four consecutive sessions (12–15 March), behaviour that is more consistent with placement price support than with pure sector dynamics. The Cleansing Notice content — placement price, size and use of funds — is not available in the data provided, which limits confirmation, and this is the key gap in the analysis.

The sector correction is real and independently confirmed but is the better explanation for the earlier leg of the decline. NST fell approximately 14% from 4 March to 8 March; EVN fell approximately 11.5% over the same period; ASB declined approximately 3%. WIA tracked these peers directionally, falling from A$0.58 to A$0.53 over the same window. The ASX200 fell approximately 4% over the 10-day period, providing a macro headwind that accounts for a portion of the absolute decline. Gold spot at US$5,018 per ounce and up 2.77% on the analysis date is consistent with the modest 2-day recovery of +2.0%, suggesting the spot price backdrop has stabilised rather than deteriorated further.

Volume on 17 March 2026 was 523,955 shares. Without an average daily volume baseline this figure cannot be assessed as elevated or normal, which is a material analytical gap. The inability to characterise whether the 17 March volume represents distribution or accumulation is a constraint on the flow analysis. Flow is assessed as probable placement-related selling in the 10–12 March window and possible stabilisation/accumulation thereafter, but neither is confirmed.

HotCopper and social media data returned no results for this period. For a micro-cap gold explorer in an active rally phase, these channels would ordinarily be expected to show some activity; their absence from the data set may reflect a data collection gap rather than genuine quiet. Social signal is assessed as irrelevant to the causal explanation of this move. No broker notes, upgrades or downgrades were identified; the absence of any broker catalyst is itself informative and consistent with the move being sector and placement driven rather than analyst-action driven.

The NST contagion sub-thesis — that NST's -18.7% single-session collapse on 12 March propagated as a sector event — is rejected. EVN moved only approximately -0.35% on that date and ASB held flat. The cross-peer data directly contradicts a broad sector contagion interpretation of the NST event, which was almost certainly company-specific. WIA's modest decline on that date (-0.7%) is consistent with background sector sentiment rather than direct contagion. The reported +202.94% single-day change in the price feed is a data artefact and carries no analytical weight; the price history is internally consistent and shows A$0.44 on both 16 and 17 March with no gap consistent with such a move.

The final judgement is that placement overhang from the 9 March capital raise and the pre-existing gold equity sector correction from the 4 March peak are jointly responsible for WIA's -10.53% 10-day decline, with the placement providing the better fit for the stock-specific portion of the move post-9 March. Confidence is moderate. The view would change materially if the Cleansing Notice reveals a placement price substantially above or below A$0.44, if a gold spot price daily history for the window shows spot declining sharply through mid-March (which would strengthen the macro driver), or if a prior placement announcement surfaces that contains terms inconsistent with the price floor hypothesis.

evidence_quality.primary_evidence: ASX official disclosure — Cleansing Notice (9 March 2026) confirming a completed capital raise is the highest-quality piece of stock-specific evidence. Peer price data for NST, EVN and ASB confirming the sector correction is reputable market data.
evidence_quality.secondary_evidence: WIA price history showing precise floor behaviour at A$0.44 post-placement is internally consistent and supports the placement price hypothesis but is an inference from price patterns rather than a confirmed placement price. Gold spot price data (+2.77% on 17 March) is reputable but only a single data point without the full 10-day history.
evidence_quality.key_gap: The content of the Cleansing Notice — specifically the placement price, number of shares issued and use of funds — has not been read from the source document. Without this, the placement price floor hypothesis at A$0.44 remains an inference rather than a confirmed fact. Additionally, gold spot price daily history for the full 10-day window is unavailable, preventing proper isolation of the macro driver from equity-specific factors. WIA average daily volume is unknown, preventing assessment of whether recent volumes represent distribution or stabilisation.

what_would_change_the_view: Cleansing Notice reveals a placement price substantially above A$0.56 or below A$0.48 — this would undermine the placement floor hypothesis and shift the balance back toward sector correction as the sole primary driver

Gold spot price daily history for 4–15 March 2026 showing a sharp intraday or multi-day spot gold decline — this would strengthen the macro driver and potentially explain the equity sector selloff without needing the placement overhang thesis

Discovery of a prior placement announcement (filed before the Cleansing Notice) showing terms, size or investor identity inconsistent with the price floor interpretation — particularly if the placement was at a significant discount to market

Confirmation that NST's 12 March collapse was driven by a sector-wide input cost or royalty event (rather than company-specific earnings or guidance) — this would partially rehabilitate the contagion sub-thesis and add a third leg to the sector driver

broker_activity.consensus_change: No broker notes, upgrades or downgrades identified in the data. Absence of broker activity is consistent with a micro/small-cap gold explorer with limited sell-side coverage. The move is not broker-driven.

goldAnalysis.executive_summary: Wia Gold Limited is a single-asset Namibian gold developer advancing the Kokoseb Gold Project, which hosts a July 2025 MRE of 2.93 Moz at 1.0 g/t Au (1.81 Moz Indicated, 1.12 Moz Inferred) with no JORC reserves declared. The September 2025 Scoping Study (±25% accuracy) returns a post-tax NPV(5%) of US$646M at US$2,600/oz gold with a 38% IRR, requiring US$414M upfront capital against a current cash balance of A$45.84M and zero debt, creating a material funding gap. A DFS is targeted for H2 CY2026 alongside Mining Licence and ECC award, positioning WIA as a near-term developer but with execution, permitting, and financing risk concentrated in the next 12–18 months. Management carries credible African gold development pedigree via Centamin, Predictive Discovery, and OreCorp.

investment_view.bull_case: At spot gold prices (~US$3,450/oz at time of study presentation), the Scoping Study NPV lifts to US$1,269M with a 60% IRR and 1.25-year payback. Deeper high-grade underground mineralisation currently being drilled could materially upgrade the resource base and mine economics ahead of DFS, while Namibia's stable, miner-friendly jurisdiction reduces sovereign risk. If DFS confirms Scoping Study economics and capital markets remain constructive, strategic acquirer or project finance interest could re-rate WIA significantly above current EV/resource multiples.
investment_view.base_case: DFS is delivered in H2 2026 broadly in line with Scoping Study parameters. Mining Licence and ECC are granted on schedule. Financing of ~US$414M is secured via a combination of project debt, streaming, and equity over 2026–2027, albeit at dilutive terms. Construction commences in 2027, first gold in 2029–2030. The project produces ~146 koz/year at LOM AISC of US$1,447/oz, generating robust free cash flow at current gold prices but leaving WIA as a mid-tier single-asset producer with limited near-term share price catalysts beyond study milestones.
investment_view.bear_case: DFS reveals material cost or capex escalation beyond Scoping Study ±25% range, or the resource is downgraded during conversion drilling. Financing proves elusive or heavily dilutive in a risk-off environment. Permitting delays extend the critical path beyond 2027. Gold price reversion toward US$2,000/oz compresses NPV to sub-US$300M, undermining project financing viability. Epangelo JV carried interest terms, if unfavourable, could further dilute project economics.
what_must_be_true: DFS confirms capital cost within ~15% of Scoping Study estimate (i.e., pre-production capex below ~US$415M) to maintain project economics.
Mining Licence and Environmental Clearance Certificate are granted by H2 2026 on schedule.
WIA successfully arranges ~US$414M in project finance and/or equity without excessive dilution to existing shareholders.
Metallurgical testwork in DFS confirms 90%+ CIL recovery across the full ore inventory including transitional and fresh ore domains.
Epangelo JV terms do not impose a material additional economic burden beyond standard state participation structures.
Gold price remains above ~US$2,000/oz through the project financing window to maintain bankable economics.
Underground drilling confirms higher-grade shoots that are additive to project NPV rather than diverting capex without commensurate return.
investment_view.monitoring_trigger: Release of DFS in H2 2026: if pre-production capex exceeds US$480M (>+15% vs Scoping Study) or LOM AISC exceeds US$1,700/oz, re-assess investment thesis immediately.

assets.name: Kokoseb Gold Project (Damaran Project)
assets.deposit_type: Sediment-hosted, structurally controlled, Pan-African Damara Orogenic Belt
assets.mining_method: Conventional open pit (drill & blast, load & haul); potential future underground
assets.study_stage: Scoping Study (September 2025, ±25% accuracy); DFS in progress, targeted H2 CY2026
technical_red_flags: No JORC Ore Reserves declared; project economics rest entirely on a Scoping Study production target (not a Feasibility Study reserve base).
18% of LOM production target is Inferred Resource, which cannot underpin Ore Reserve estimates or bankable financing.
Scoping Study has ±25% cost accuracy — pre-production capex of US$414.3M could realistically range from US$311M to US$518M.
Oxide/transitional/fresh ore metallurgical characterisation not disclosed; recoveries across ore domains not confirmed.
Water supply relies on multiple sources including untested Okombahe scheme and fossil groundwater (OmAP); hydrogeology modelling not yet complete.
Power supply requires construction of a new 95 km 66 kV grid spur — infrastructure lead time and cost risk.
Epangelo JV carried interest, free-carry provisions, and profit share terms not publicly disclosed, creating residual economic dilution risk.
No FX sensitivity disclosed despite AUD-denominated costs and USD-denominated revenues creating a natural FX exposure.
No deleterious elements or refractory ore characterisation disclosed.
Annual production schedule not available in tabular form; year-by-year strip ratio, grade, and capex profile not verifiable.
information_gaps: Epangelo JV ownership split and carried interest terms.
Detailed annual production schedule (grade, strip ratio, throughput, sustaining capex by year).
Oxide vs transitional vs fresh ore proportions and domain-specific metallurgical recoveries.
FX assumption used in Scoping Study economics.
Geotechnical outcomes from DFS drilling (pit slope angles, geotechnical domains).
Hydrogeology model and confirmed water supply allocation from NamWater.
Power infrastructure capex included vs excluded from pre-production capex estimate.
Corporate tax rate confirmation and any applicable withholding tax on dividends.
Details of any NSR or royalty agreements beyond the statutory 3% ad valorem royalty and 1% export levy.
DFS contractor selection and procurement strategy.
Current market capitalisation and share count to calculate EV/resource and P/NAV.

valuation.valuation_notes: Scoping Study post-tax NPV(5%) of US$646M at US$2,600/oz gold (38% IRR, 1.8-year payback) and US$1,269M at US$3,450/oz (60% IRR, 1.25-year payback). At approximately US$3,450/oz spot (Feb 2026), the NPV sensitivity of US$73.3M per US$100/oz implies a further meaningful uplift above the spot case NPV. No JORC reserves exist, so EV/reserve oz and P/NAV vs IC NAV cannot be calculated. Market cap, share count, and EV are not available in the corpus, preventing calculation of EV/resource oz or P/NAV. The Scoping Study NPV should be treated as an upper-bound indicative value given ±25% cost accuracy and the absence of a DFS. The DFS NPV is the critical upcoming catalyst.

peer_group: Predictive Discovery Limited (ASX:PDI) — Bankan Gold Project, Guinea
OreCorp Limited (ASX:ORR) — Nyanzaga Gold Project, Tanzania
West African Resources (ASX:WAF) — Sanbrado Mine, Burkina Faso
Resolved Gold (ASX:RGD) — Namibian gold developer peers
B2Gold Corp (TSX:BTO) — Otjikoto Mine, Namibia (operating comparator)
peer_frame.relative_valuation_comment: No peer valuation data is available in the corpus. WIA's Scoping Study NPV of US$646M at US$2,600/oz for a 2.93 Moz resource base compares favourably in absolute terms to similarly staged African gold developers, but the absence of Ore Reserves, ±25% cost accuracy, and a US$414M funding requirement are significant discounts to NAV for investors. At current spot gold prices (~US$3,450/oz), the project economics are materially more compelling. Formal peer EV/resource oz and P/NAV comparisons cannot be made without current market capitalisation data.

top_failure_modes: Failure to raise ~US$414M in project financing — funding gap is existential; any equity component at current stage is likely heavily dilutive.
DFS reveals capex materially above Scoping Study (±25% range implies US$311M–US$518M pre-production capex), impairing project economics and bankability.
Permitting delay or denial — Mining Licence and ECC are critical path items with H2 2026 target; any regulatory setback pushes first gold to 2030+.
Gold price reversion to sub-US$2,000/oz collapsing project NPV and preventing project financing.
Metallurgical test work in DFS reveals lower recoveries in transitional/fresh ore domains, increasing processing costs and reducing LOM gold production.
Epangelo JV terms, once disclosed, impose a material free-carry or profit participation that reduces WIA's effective project economics.
Resource downgrade during DFS infill drilling, particularly conversion of Inferred to lower-confidence categories.
technical_red_flags: No JORC Ore Reserves — project is pre-reserve; economics cannot be used to support reserve-based financing without DFS completion.
Scoping Study cost accuracy of ±25% — US$414M capex could reach US$518M at the high end.
18% of LOM production target from Inferred Resources — cannot underpin financing.
Metallurgical characterisation of ore domains (oxide/transitional/fresh) not disclosed; recovery risk across full ore inventory.
Water supply infrastructure not confirmed — hydrogeology modelling is incomplete and NamWater allocation application is pending.
New 95 km power line spur required — infrastructure construction risk and potential capex item.
No FX sensitivity analysis disclosed despite material AUD/USD exposure.
Epangelo JV economic terms undisclosed.
Reconciliation risk flagged in corpus
Metallurgical complexity: refractory ore
Operational risk: dilution pressure referenced
Accounting risk: capitalised stripping treatment mentioned
hard_risk_flags: US$414M funding gap with only A$45.84M cash on hand — company is 'fully financed to DFS' only, not to production.
Scoping Study explicitly states 'there is no certainty that the Company will be able to raise that amount of funding when needed' — company's own disclosure.
Funding 'may only be available on terms that may be dilutive to or otherwise affect the value of the Company's existing shares' — dilution risk is company-acknowledged.
No JORC Ore Reserves declared; project cannot be financed on a bankable reserve basis until DFS completion at earliest.
Concentration risk: flagship asset exceeds 80% of NAV
information_gaps: Epangelo JV ownership percentage and economic terms (carried interest, free-carry, profit sharing).
Current market capitalisation, share count, and enterprise value.
FX assumption used in Scoping Study (AUD/USD rate).
Year-by-year production schedule with grade, strip ratio, throughput, and sustaining capex.
Ore domain breakdown (oxide/transitional/fresh) and domain-specific metallurgical recoveries.
Geotechnical outcomes (pit slope angles) from DFS drilling.
Water supply confirmation from NamWater — volume and timing.
DFS contractor and procurement approach.
Corporate finance strategy for the ~US$414M funding requirement (debt, equity, streaming mix).
Withholding tax on dividends or inter-company payments under Namibian tax code.
Any NSR or royalty encumbrances beyond statutory royalties.
Dilution history and track record of capital raises at WIA.

goldAnalysis.management_assessment: WIA's management team carries strong credibility for an ASX-listed African gold developer. Josef El-Raghy (Non-Executive Chairman) guided Centamin's Sukari mine from exploration to one of Africa's largest gold operations. Incoming CEO Henk Diederichs (effective 01 Feb 2026) was COO at Predictive Discovery (Bankan, Guinea) and CEO/MD at OreCorp (Nyanzaga, Tanzania), both directly comparable development-stage African gold projects. Andrew Pardey (NED) is current MD of Predictive Discovery and ex-CEO of Centamin. Exploration Manager Pierrick Couderc is credited with the original Kokoseb discovery in 2021 and has a strong discovery track record in West Africa. Board and management hold 9% of shares, providing meaningful but not dominant alignment. The team has demonstrated capital discipline by divesting non-core Côte d'Ivoire assets to focus on Namibia. The primary execution risk is whether the team can attract and manage the ~US$414M project financing process, which is a materially different skill set from exploration and development — no specific financing track record at this scale is documented in the corpus. Incoming CEO tenure is very short (effective 01 Feb 2026 at analysis date); his integration and strategic direction are as yet unproven in this role.

recommendation.reason: WIA's Kokoseb project is technically credible — a 2.93 Moz resource at 1.0 g/t Au in a stable jurisdiction, with straightforward open pit mining and CIL metallurgy, compelling Scoping Study economics (38% IRR at US$2,600/oz, 60% at US$3,450/oz), and an experienced management team. However, the investment case is currently gated by three unresolved binary risks: (1) DFS outcome (H2 2026, ±25% Scoping Study accuracy leaves substantial economic uncertainty); (2) US$414M funding requirement against A$45.84M cash — the company itself acknowledges no certainty of raising this capital; and (3) Mining Licence and ECC award, both on a H2 2026 timeline. The stock is best approached as a DFS/permit/financing catalyst play. Re-engagement is warranted upon DFS release if pre-production capex is confirmed below US$480M, AISC below US$1,600/oz, and evidence of credible project financing progress is visible. The current risk/reward profile does not support immediate capital commitment given the volume and timing of unresolved material uncertainties.

evidence.label: MRE July 2025
evidence.finding: Total MRE of 2.93 Moz at 1.0 g/t Au (0.5 g/t cut-off): Indicated 54.2 Mt at 1.04 g/t for 1.81 Moz; Inferred 35 Mt at 0.99 g/t for 1.12 Moz. No JORC Ore Reserves declared.

evidence.label: Scoping Study — Economics
evidence.finding: Post-tax NPV(5%) US$646M at US$2,600/oz (IRR 38%, payback 1.8 years); US$1,269M at US$3,450/oz (IRR 60%, payback 1.25 years). Pre-production capex US$414.3M. LOM AISC US$1,447/oz. Mine life 11.4 years. Average LOM production ~146 koz/year. Study accuracy ±25%.

evidence.label: Scoping Study — Production Target
evidence.finding: LOM production target 58.9 Mt at 0.97 g/t Au containing 1.83 Moz Au. 82% Indicated, 18% Inferred. Inferred <6% of first 7 years production schedule.

evidence.label: Scoping Study — Costs
evidence.finding: C1 cash cost US$1,317/oz; LOM AISC US$1,447/oz; Years 1–5 AISC US$1,265/oz. Mining US$724/oz, Processing & Tailings US$504/oz, G&A US$85/oz, Royalties US$104/oz, Sustaining Capital & Closure US$25/oz.

evidence.label: Balance Sheet — December 2025
evidence.finding: Cash balance A$45.84M, zero debt, liquidity runway 7.40 quarters (~22 months) as at 31 December 2025.
evidence.source: WIA Quarterly Report / Financial Statements, December 2025

evidence.label: Funding Risk Disclosure
evidence.finding: Scoping Study explicitly states no certainty company can raise ~US$414M required; funding may be dilutive to or otherwise affect value of existing shares.

evidence.label: Mining Licence Application
evidence.finding: Mining Licence Application submitted to Namibia Ministry of Mines and Energy in October 2025. ESIA nearing completion, draft circulated for public comment, submission anticipated Q1 2026. ECC and Mining Licence award targeted H2 2026.
evidence.source: WIA ASX Announcement / DFS update, Q4 2025 – Q1 2026

evidence.label: Metallurgy
evidence.finding: Conventional CIL plant at 5.25 Mtpa, P80 63µm grind. Preliminary testwork >90% recovery. LOM average 90% recovery. Ore domain breakdown not disclosed.

evidence.label: Fiscal Terms — Namibia
evidence.finding: 37.5% corporate tax rate for gold mining; 3% ad valorem royalty on gross gold market value; 1% export levy; 15% VAT with input credit recovery. No CGT on licence disposals.
evidence.source: Kokoseb Scoping Study / WIA corporate documents

evidence.label: Jurisdiction
evidence.finding: Namibia ranked 22/82 Fraser Institute 2024 policy attractiveness survey (above all Australian states except WA), 4th for Investment Attractiveness in Africa. Stable democracy, independent judiciary.
evidence.source: Fraser Institute 2024 Survey, cited in WIA presentation

evidence.label: Management — Key Personnel
evidence.finding: Josef El-Raghy (Chair, ex-Centamin CEO); Henk Diederichs (CEO effective 01 Feb 2026, ex-Predictive Discovery COO, ex-OreCorp CEO); Andrew Pardey (NED, current Predictive Discovery MD, ex-Centamin CEO); Pierrick Couderc (Exploration Manager, discovered Kokoseb 2021). Board & management hold 9% of shares.
evidence.source: WIA ASX Announcements and company presentations, 2025–2026

evidence.label: Epangelo JV
evidence.finding: Damaran Project held under joint venture with state-owned Epangelo Mining. Specific economic terms (ownership split, carried interest, profit participation) not disclosed in available documents.

evidence.label: Divested Côte d'Ivoire assets
evidence.finding: Non-core Côte d'Ivoire assets divested to Santa Fe Minerals Limited (ASX:SFM) in November 2025. Non-cash impairment of CIV E&E assets to be recognised in December 2025 half-year accounts.

evidence.label: Water Supply
evidence.finding: Project water demand ~1.1 Mm³/year. Okombahe Water Supply Scheme (933,000 m³/a potential) and Ozondati scheme (~220,000 m³/a) identified as primary sources. Application to NamWater for up to 1.5 Mm³/year submitted. Hydrogeology modelling imminent.

evidence.label: Power Supply
evidence.finding: Project requires ~31 MW from Namibian grid via new 95 km 66 kV spur from Omburu substation. Average power cost US$0.117/kWh over LOM.

evidence.label: NPV Sensitivity
evidence.finding: Each US$100/oz change in gold price adds approximately US$73.3M to post-tax NPV(5%) at base case US$2,600/oz.

goldAnalysis.verdict: Wia Gold Limited is a single-asset Namibian gold developer advancing the Kokoseb Gold Project, which hosts a July 2025 MRE of 2.93 Moz at 1.0 g/t Au (1.81 Moz Indicated, 1.12 Moz Inferred) with no JORC reserves declared. The September 2025 Scoping Study (±25% accuracy) returns a post-tax NPV(5%) of US$646M at US$2,600/oz gold with a 38% IRR, requiring US$414M upfront capital against a current cash balance of A$45.84M and zero debt, creating a material funding gap. A DFS is targeted for H2 CY2026 alongside Mining Licence and ECC award, positioning WIA as a near-term developer but with execution, permitting, and financing risk concentrated in the next 12–18 months. Management carries credible African gold development pedigree via Centamin, Predictive Discovery, and OreCorp.

hypothesis.bull: At spot gold prices (~US$3,450/oz at time of study presentation), the Scoping Study NPV lifts to US$1,269M with a 60% IRR and 1.25-year payback. Deeper high-grade underground mineralisation currently being drilled could materially upgrade the resource base and mine economics ahead of DFS, while Namibia's stable, miner-friendly jurisdiction reduces sovereign risk. If DFS confirms Scoping Study economics and capital markets remain constructive, strategic acquirer or project finance interest could re-rate WIA significantly above current EV/resource multiples.
hypothesis.bear: DFS reveals material cost or capex escalation beyond Scoping Study ±25% range, or the resource is downgraded during conversion drilling. Financing proves elusive or heavily dilutive in a risk-off environment. Permitting delays extend the critical path beyond 2027. Gold price reversion toward US$2,000/oz compresses NPV to sub-US$300M, undermining project financing viability. Epangelo JV carried interest terms, if unfavourable, could further dilute project economics.

goldAnalysis.monitoring_trigger: Release of DFS in H2 2026: if pre-production capex exceeds US$480M (>+15% vs Scoping Study) or LOM AISC exceeds US$1,700/oz, re-assess investment thesis immediately.
